reviews.review_msg: doesn't get any better than this

As the other ratings say, THE best professor in the entire school. His lectures are actually enjoyable to go to in spite of it being at 8:30AM. His clarity in teaching is the best of any professor I have ever seen. Yes, course material can be difficult at times, but I do not attribute it in the slightest to Dr. Mulholland's teaching ability.

If Jamie has one fan, it is me. If Jamie has ten fans, I am one of them. If Jamie has a thousand fans, I am one of them. If Jamie has a million fans, I am one of them. If Jamie has zero fans, then I am dead. If the world is against Jamie, then I am against the world. If Jamie has haters, I am none of them. I will love Jamie until my last breath.
Probably the BEST professor in SFU
Amazing prof, reasonable tests, make sures the test questions you have either seen before or will be able to do if u do all HW and study.
Jamie actually is the goat man, he explains everything so clearly and the midterms were pretty fair.
Best math prof you can find at SFU.
The best math prof you can find at SFU
Had him for math151 and also for math152 prime. The prime section was the best course out of all my courses so far. Office hours were the best. Goated.
Two simple words. THE GOAT!!!
Honestly the best professor at SFU

He goes through his notes pretty fast and acts like the students in front of him can do 10 steps of algebra in their mind without him showing the work. His exams are very difficult and are nothing compared to the hw and webassigns he gives.

He's a great prof. As all of the other reviews say he is very good at explaining and made this course very enjoyable. This course was on the easier side for me though as I've taken calc in highschool but it doesn't change the fact that he's an incredible professor.

Goated for a reason. I would suggest not skipping lectures, but if you absolutely have to, you can catch up using canvas. Everything is very clear, both the structure of the course and the material. Just listen to him and ask questions if any. Course mirrors the Stewart textbook so no surprise curveballs. Easy course (compared to other math)

I took calculus last year in high school and hated it, but somehow Jamie made me enjoy it this year (and that says a lot). Great, friendly professor, has office hours in the calc workshop which was helpful for me, takes lots of time explaining concepts in depth. Make sure to do your homework and you'll be fine.
goat of sfu

Jamie Mulholland is the calculus GOAT. He's very approachable and his YouTube videos with great explanations helped my friends and I do well in the course. Midterms and Final were fair as long as you completed the practice tests. Don't miss a chance to take math with this man!! You WILL NOT regret it

Jamie is an amazing professor. His explanations are super clear and he makes everything very easy to understand. He is very organized and gives you lots of practice tests for the midterms and the final. I would definitely recommend taking a course with him if you have the chance. He made the class very fun.

Overall, an amazing professor, his video lectures are absolutely amazing and really easy to understand. His midterms and finals can be challenging, if you haven't taken calculus before, but he provides you will lots of practice midterms/finals. His web assign/seminar assignments are also free marks.

Hes just the goat. He makes math enjoyable and fun somehow. Lots of quizzes which are straight from the homework. Midterms and final were fair, just do the practice exams and the practice. I wouldve loved attending lectures if it wasnt at 830am. Youtube videos came in clutch! Loved this class

Listen. I am not even taking his Math 251 course, im with another professor. However this man IS teaching my the content through his youtube videos better than my in person lectures. I can only imagine how great he when you actually take his class. 10/10 I would take a class with him in the future.

Dr.M is by far the best teacher I have ever had across all subjects. His notes are unbelievably organized and well written, lectures are easy to understand and well paced, exams are fair and no trick questions. Had him for both Calc 1 and Calc 2 but he is even extremely knowledgeable on topics like linear algebra and discrete math too. 10/10

Dr. Mulholland is an amazing instructor and likely one of the very best professors at SFU. His lectures are so clear and he explains complicated concepts in a way that is easy to understand. I watched all of his videos for MATH 152 and attended all of his lectures in Spring 2024. His exams were very fair. Inspired me to do a math minor!
Jamie's classes are top-notch - he explains concepts clearly and has great online resources. Using unstuck study and staying engaged made this course a breeze.

If I could give 10 stars i would. Best professor at SFU hands down. Super helpful, no question you ask is dumb question, respects all his students, and gives great analogies to help understand the material. Had him for 2 classes and it's safe to say Jamie knows his stuff and knows how to teach it.
The goat of the math department.
THE BEST!

Calculus is hard, Jamie makes it easy. Rundown: His midterms are easy to ace if you don't make mistakes and actually attend lectures. Some back things are that you have to pay for webassign. People complain about assignments due weekly but I feel like it's necessary for students to understand the material. 5/5 is too low of a rating imo.
The GOAT of SFU Math Department.

Jamie himself is fine as a lecturer, especially since he has the entire course as a youtube playlist. However, there's an insane amount of weekly homework and quizzes which are barely worth anything. If you're unfamiliar with any pre-cal concepts you're going to find yourself struggling to keep up. Overall I don't get the hype.

You can tell Jamie is an experienced prof. His course and teaching are very organized and clear. He knows what he is talking about and he cares for his students' learning. I loved that he had youtube videos for the whole course, so you can go back for a better understanding or watch in case you slept in.
gave me an extension cuz i forgot also great overall
Jamie is a good teacher. I highly recommend taking it. The only detriment is the early lecture time.

Jamie is very knowledgable and explains material exceptionally well during lectures and in his office hours, he knows what he's doing. As for course material, make sure you practise his assignments and notes lots so you can receive good grades on quizzes and exams. Trust me, you won't regret taking a course with Jamie.

So glad I got into Dr. Mulholland's class for my first year. Heard lots of great things about him and everything was true! He covers content at a good pace and has Youtube videos on everything if you don't understand. Explains everything very well and cares a lot about his students.
